package main import ( "bytes" "errors" "fmt" "html" "mime" "strings" "time" ) // BuildFeedbackEmail builds an HTML email body containing user-submitted feedback. // It returns a full RFC 5322 message (headers + body) suitable for smtp.SendMail. func BuildFeedbackEmail(fromAddr string, toAddr string, userName string, subject string, feedbackContent string) (emailBody string, err error) { fromAddr = strings.TrimSpace(fromAddr) toAddr = strings.TrimSpace(toAddr) userName = strings.TrimSpace(userName) subject = strings.TrimSpace(subject) feedbackContent = strings.TrimSpace(feedbackContent) if fromAddr == "" { return "", errors.New("fromAddr is required") } if toAddr == "" { return "", errors.New("toAddr is required") } if subject == "" { subject = "User Feedback" } // Sanitize user-provided content for safe HTML rendering. escName := html.EscapeString(userName) escSubject := html.EscapeString(subject) escContent := html.EscapeString(feedbackContent) // Preserve newlines in feedback content. escContent = strings.ReplaceAll(escContent, "\r\n", "\n") escContent = strings.ReplaceAll(escContent, "\n", "
\n") now := time.Now().Format(time.RFC1123Z) htmlBody := fmt.Sprintf(` %s

New User Feedback

From (user) %s
Subject %s
Feedback %s
Submitted At %s
`, escSubject, escName, escSubject, escContent, html.EscapeString(now)) // Construct RFC 5322 message with MIME headers for HTML content. // Use mime package to encode the Subject header safely. var msg bytes.Buffer msg.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("From: %s\r\n", fromAddr)) msg.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("To: %s\r\n", toAddr)) msg.WriteString("MIME-Version: 1.0\r\n") msg.WriteString("Content-Type: text/html; charset=UTF-8\r\n") msg.WriteString("Content-Transfer-Encoding: 8bit\r\n") msg.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("Date: %s\r\n", now)) msg.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("Subject: %s\r\n", mime.QEncoding.Encode("utf-8", subject))) msg.WriteString("\r\n") msg.WriteString(htmlBody) return msg.String(), nil } func main() { // Demo usage (does not send email; it only builds the message). email, err := BuildFeedbackEmail( "no-reply@example.com", "support@example.com", "alice", "App crashes on launch", "Hi support,\n\nThe app crashes immediately after I open it.\nSteps:\n1) Tap icon\n2) Crash\n\nThanks!", ) if err != nil { panic(err) } fmt.Println(email) }

package main import ( "bytes" "errors" "fmt" "html" "mime" "strings" ) // BuildFeedbackEmail collects user feedback information and generates an HTML-formatted email. // It returns the full RFC 5322 message (headers + body) ready to be passed to smtp.SendMail. func BuildFeedbackEmail(fromAddr string, toAddr string, userName string, subject string, feedbackContent string) (emailBody string, err error) { fromAddr = strings.TrimSpace(fromAddr) toAddr = strings.TrimSpace(toAddr) userName = strings.TrimSpace(userName) subject = strings.TrimSpace(subject) feedbackContent = strings.TrimSpace(feedbackContent) if fromAddr == "" || toAddr == "" { return "", errors.New("fromAddr and toAddr must not be empty") } if subject == "" { subject = "User Feedback" } if userName == "" { userName = "Anonymous" } if feedbackContent == "" { feedbackContent = "(empty feedback)" } // Escape user input for safe HTML embedding. escName := html.EscapeString(userName) escSubject := html.EscapeString(subject) escContent := html.EscapeString(feedbackContent) // Preserve newlines in feedback content. escContent = strings.ReplaceAll(escContent, "\r\n", "\n") escContent = strings.ReplaceAll(escContent, "\r", "\n") escContent = strings.ReplaceAll(escContent, "\n", "
\n") htmlBody := fmt.Sprintf(` %s

User Feedback Submission

Username: %s
Subject: %s
Feedback: %s
`, escSubject, escName, escSubject, escContent) // Build RFC 5322 message with MIME headers. // Encode the subject header properly (handles non-ASCII). var msg bytes.Buffer msg.WriteString("From: " + fromAddr + "\r\n") msg.WriteString("To: " + toAddr + "\r\n") msg.WriteString("Subject: " + mime.QEncoding.Encode("utf-8", subject) + "\r\n") msg.WriteString("MIME-Version: 1.0\r\n") msg.WriteString(`Content-Type: text/html; charset="UTF-8"` + "\r\n") msg.WriteString("Content-Transfer-Encoding: 8bit\r\n") msg.WriteString("\r\n") msg.WriteString(htmlBody) return msg.String(), nil } func main() { // Example usage (prints the email content). In real usage, pass the returned // message to smtp.SendMail. email, err := BuildFeedbackEmail( "no-reply@example.com", "support@example.com", "alice", "App feedback: settings page", "Hi support,\nThe settings page is confusing.\nPlease improve the layout.\nThanks!", ) if err != nil { panic(err) } fmt.Println(email) }

package main import ( "bytes" "errors" "fmt" "html" "mime" "strings" "time" ) // BuildFeedbackEmail builds an RFC 5322 email message with an HTML body containing // the user-submitted feedback details. The returned string can be passed directly // to smtp.SendMail as the message body. func BuildFeedbackEmail(fromAddr string, toAddr string, userName string, subject string, feedbackContent string) (emailBody string, err error) { fromAddr = strings.TrimSpace(fromAddr) toAddr = strings.TrimSpace(toAddr) userName = strings.TrimSpace(userName) subject = strings.TrimSpace(subject) feedbackContent = strings.TrimSpace(feedbackContent) if fromAddr == "" || toAddr == "" { return "", errors.New("fromAddr and toAddr are required") } if subject == "" { subject = "User Feedback" } if userName == "" { userName = "Anonymous" } if feedbackContent == "" { feedbackContent = "(no content provided)" } // HTML-escape all user-provided fields to avoid HTML injection. escUser := html.EscapeString(userName) escSubject := html.EscapeString(subject) // Preserve newlines in feedback content. escContent := html.EscapeString(feedbackContent) escContent = strings.ReplaceAll(escContent, "\r\n", "\n") escContent = strings.ReplaceAll(escContent, "\r", "\n") escContent = strings.ReplaceAll(escContent, "\n", "
\n") // Basic HTML email body. htmlBody := fmt.Sprintf(` %s

User Feedback

User Name: %s
Subject: %s
Submitted At: %s

Feedback Content

%s
`, escSubject, escUser, escSubject, time.Now().Format(time.RFC1123Z), escContent) // Construct RFC 5322 headers + MIME for HTML. var msg bytes.Buffer // Standard headers. msg.WriteString("From: " + fromAddr + "\r\n") msg.WriteString("To: " + toAddr + "\r\n") msg.WriteString("Subject: " + mime.QEncoding.Encode("utf-8", subject) + "\r\n") msg.WriteString("MIME-Version: 1.0\r\n") msg.WriteString("Content-Type: text/html; charset=UTF-8\r\n") msg.WriteString("Content-Transfer-Encoding: 8bit\r\n") msg.WriteString("\r\n") // End of headers msg.WriteString(htmlBody) return msg.String(), nil } func main() { // Demo usage (prints the generated email message). email, err := BuildFeedbackEmail( "no-reply@example.com", "support@example.com", "alice", "App crashes on login", "Hi support,\n\nThe app crashes when I tap 'Login'.\nSteps:\n1) Open app\n2) Tap Login\n\nThanks!", ) if err != nil { panic(err) } fmt.Println(email) }

package main import ( "bytes" "fmt" "html" "mime" "net/smtp" "strings" "time" ) // BuildFeedbackEmail constructs an RFC 5322 email message with an HTML body. // It returns the full email message bytes as a string (headers + body). func BuildFeedbackEmail(fromAddr string, toAddr string, userName string, subject string, feedbackContent string) (emailBody string, err error) { fromAddr = strings.TrimSpace(fromAddr) toAddr = strings.TrimSpace(toAddr) if fromAddr == "" { return "", fmt.Errorf("fromAddr is required") } if toAddr == "" { return "", fmt.Errorf("toAddr is required") } // RFC 2047 encode the Subject for non-ASCII safety. encodedSubject := mime.QEncoding.Encode("utf-8", subject) // Escape user-provided values to avoid HTML injection. escName := html.EscapeString(userName) escSubject := html.EscapeString(subject) escContent := html.EscapeString(feedbackContent) // Preserve line breaks in feedback content. escContent = strings.ReplaceAll(escContent, "\r\n", "\n") escContent = strings.ReplaceAll(escContent, "\n", "
\n") now := time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC1123Z) htmlBody := fmt.Sprintf(` User Feedback

New User Feedback

Username: %s
Subject: %s
Submitted at (UTC): %s
Feedback: %s
This email was generated automatically by the feedback system.
`, escName, escSubject, now, escContent) // Construct the message with headers. // net/smtp expects CRLF line endings. var msg bytes.Buffer writeHeader := func(k, v string) { msg.WriteString(k) msg.WriteString(": ") msg.WriteString(v) msg.WriteString("\r\n") } writeHeader("From", fromAddr) writeHeader("To", toAddr) writeHeader("Subject", encodedSubject) writeHeader("MIME-Version", "1.0") writeHeader("Content-Type", `text/html; charset="UTF-8"`) writeHeader("Content-Transfer-Encoding", "7bit") msg.WriteString("\r\n") // End of headers msg.WriteString(htmlBody) msg.WriteString("\r\n") // Reference net/smtp so it's clear we're constructing content intended for smtp.SendMail. _ = smtp.SendMail return msg.String(), nil } func main() { email, err := BuildFeedbackEmail( "no-reply@example.com", "support@example.com", "alice", "App crashes on launch", "Hi support,\n\nThe app crashes immediately after I open it.\nSteps:\n1) Tap icon\n2) Crash\n\nThanks!", ) if err != nil { panic(err) } // Print the generated email content (headers + body). fmt.Println(email) }
